name: Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 12 oz bittersweet chocolate (not unsweetened) or semisweet chocolate, chopped
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
1. Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 300°F.
2. Line the bottom of a 9-inch-diameter springform pan with waxed paper, creating a 2 3/4-inch-high side barrier.
3. Spray the waxed paper with nonstick vegetable oil spray.
4. Using an electric mixer on low speed, beat cream cheese and sugar in a large bowl until smooth.
5. Gradually mix in cocoa powder.
6. Beat in eggs one at a time.
7. Gradually add whipping cream, coffee liqueur, Frangelico, and vanilla extract, beating until the mixture is smooth.
8. Transfer filling to the prepared pan.
9. Bake for 1 hour and 30 minutes until the outer 3 inches of the cake puffs and the center is gently set but moist-looking.
10. Cool on a rack.
11. Cover and refrigerate until the cake is well chilled, at least 6 hours (or up to 24 hours).
Chocolate Glaze:
12. In a heavy saucepan over medium heat, boil coffee liqueur, Frangelico, and rum until reduced to 1/4 cup, about 3 minutes.
13. Reduce heat to low and add chopped chocolate, whipping cream, and butter. Stir until the mixture is smooth.
14. Allow the glaze to cool until it thickens but remains pourable, stirring occasionally for about 45 minutes.
15. Using a small, sharp knife, loosen the cake around the pan sides.
16. Release the pan sides and pour the chocolate glaze over the cake, spreading smoothly over the top and sides to cover completely.
17. Chill the cake until the chocolate is firm, at least 2 hours.
18. Invert the cake onto a serving platter, peel off any remaining paper, smooth the top with a warm knife, and cut into wedges for serving.
